_instructions.purpose: This is the lightweight loader file. At the start of every session the user will say 'access my MemoryAccess repository'. Claude should then fetch this file via GitHub MCP and acknowledge it is loaded. The main knowledge base URL is embedded — fetch chunks directly when needed.
how_to_load: 1. At session start: user says 'access my MemoryAccess repository'. Fetch session_loader.json via GitHub MCP (owner: welwordion-sys, repo: MemoryAccess). Acknowledge it is loaded. Do not fetch the knowledge base yet.
2. When the user makes a request: scan the top-level index to identify relevant domains.
3. Suggest which domain(s) and sub-index keys seem relevant. Wait for user confirmation.
4. After confirmation: fetch knowledge_base.json via GitHub MCP and load ONLY the confirmed chunk(s) by key path.
5. After reading a chunk: summarize what was loaded and proceed with the task.
6. Never speculatively read additional chunks unless the user requests it.
_instructions.note: The index below reflects the top-level domains and their sub-index summaries. Sub-topic details live only in the knowledge base.

meta._summary: Project-level metadata: author, dates, versioning, global tags.

research._summary: Core research areas, sources, and findings.

concepts._summary: Key terms, definitions, and conceptual frameworks.

entities._summary: Named entities: people, organizations, locations, artifacts.

timeline._summary: Chronological events and milestones relevant to the project.

notes._summary: Working notes, drafts, and session logs.
